historicalConfiguration.nonAuthorityStatement: The retired configuration is historical evidence only, not an order or Release Unit membership source.

manifestComparison.nonAuthorityStatement: The retired configuration is historical evidence only, not an order or Release Unit membership source.

mismatches.observedBehavior: The workspace manifest declares the publishable vexil-codegen-py crate, but .vexilbot.toml has no release.crates.vexil-codegen-py entry.
packages/runtime-go has a Go module manifest, but .vexilbot.toml has no package or dependency-order entry for it.
packages/runtime-py has a Python project manifest, but .vexilbot.toml has no package or dependency-order entry for it.

responsibilities.description: Prepare a release candidate, changelog input, and CI preconditions before a human approval route.

historicalEvidence.observedBehavior: The retired release section named git-cliff and required CI while disabling automatic release.
The current workflow plans tagged release work and describes release-artifact preparation.

responsibilities.failureImpact: A release can be prepared without recorded checks or a coherent changelog input.

advisoryDisposition.rationale: Release preparation signals remain a human-owned, non-authoritative checklist while continuity blocks privileged release work.
advisoryDisposition.failureBehavior: Record the unavailable advisory checklist and defer rather than infer approval from CI or historical automation.

fallback.noPrivilegeBoundary: The fallback has no privileged access and cannot authorize release activity.

advisoryDisposition.nonAuthorityBoundary: It cannot select scope or version, accept risk, approve a Manifest, satisfy a privileged gate, or trigger publication.

manualProcedure.noPrivilegeBoundary: The procedure has no privileged access and cannot authorize release activity.

responsibilities.description: Identify and communicate package dependency ordering for a prospective release without deriving authoritative membership from retired configuration.

historicalEvidence.observedBehavior: Retired crate entries recorded depends_on relationships and a TypeScript post-publish step.
The public history records an explicit addition of the Go code generator and Go runtime to the retired configuration, corroborating that the configuration was incomplete and changed over time.
The workspace currently lists a Python code generator alongside the Rust crates.

responsibilities.failureImpact: A later release can silently omit or incorrectly order a maintained unit.

advisoryDisposition.rationale: Dependency ordering advice is a human comparison of maintained manifests and is not a Release Set decision.
advisoryDisposition.failureBehavior: Record unavailable ordering advice and defer; do not derive membership or order from retired configuration.

fallback.noPrivilegeBoundary: The fallback has no privileged access and cannot authorize release activity.

advisoryDisposition.nonAuthorityBoundary: It cannot select scope or version, accept risk, approve a Manifest, satisfy a privileged gate, or trigger publication.

manualProcedure.noPrivilegeBoundary: The procedure has no privileged access and cannot authorize release activity.

responsibilities.description: Interpret release tag formats and prevent unreviewed tag-driven release effects.

historicalEvidence.observedBehavior: The retired configuration specified a crate-version tag format.
The release workflow is triggered by vexilc version tags.

responsibilities.failureImpact: An incorrect tag may initiate an unintended build or public announcement path.

responsibilities.description: Coordinate registry and release publication only after separately authorized approval and external-control gates.

historicalEvidence.observedBehavior: Retired release entries named crates.io publication targets.
The current workflow publishes the TypeScript runtime from a tag trigger.
The current release workflow describes artifact upload and GitHub Release creation.

responsibilities.failureImpact: A package, artifact, or release record can be published without complete custody and recovery proof.

responsibilities.description: Triage incoming issues and pull requests into a maintainer review path.

historicalEvidence.observedBehavior: The retired configuration limited triage to maintainers and collaborators.
Current issue-template configuration routes public support and reporting entry points.

responsibilities.failureImpact: Contributor reports can remain untriaged or be handled without an accountable route.

advisoryDisposition.rationale: A declared, not-deployed triage contract keeps routing advice reviewable without granting repository-wide write authority.
advisoryDisposition.failureBehavior: If the contract is unavailable, the Repository Administrator manually triages or defers with public evidence.

fallback.noPrivilegeBoundary: The fallback has no privileged access and cannot authorize release activity.

advisoryDisposition.nonAuthorityBoundary: It cannot select scope or version, accept risk, approve a Manifest, satisfy a privileged gate, or trigger publication.

automation.trigger: A maintainer invokes the declared advisory contract against issue or pull-request metadata.

responsibilities.description: Apply consistent path and keyword labels to issues and pull requests as advisory metadata.

historicalEvidence.observedBehavior: The retired configuration mapped paths and keywords to labels.

responsibilities.failureImpact: Work can lose routing context and maintainers can miss affected ownership surfaces.

advisoryDisposition.rationale: A declared, not-deployed labeling contract preserves advisory routing metadata with only issue and pull-request write intents.
advisoryDisposition.failureBehavior: If the contract is unavailable, the Repository Administrator manually labels or defers with public evidence.

fallback.noPrivilegeBoundary: The fallback has no privileged access and cannot authorize release activity.

advisoryDisposition.nonAuthorityBoundary: It cannot select scope or version, accept risk, approve a Manifest, satisfy a privileged gate, or trigger publication.

automation.trigger: A maintainer invokes the declared advisory contract against issue or pull-request metadata.

responsibilities.description: Acknowledge first pull requests and issues without representing an approval or support commitment.

historicalEvidence.observedBehavior: The retired configuration contained first-PR and issue welcome messages.

responsibilities.failureImpact: New contributors receive no expected-review or reproduction guidance.

advisoryDisposition.rationale: Automatic welcome messaging is retired to avoid implying approval, support commitment, or automation authority.
advisoryDisposition.failureBehavior: There is no automatic acknowledgement; the owner may manually respond or defer with public evidence.

fallback.noPrivilegeBoundary: The fallback has no privileged access and cannot authorize release activity.

advisoryDisposition.nonAuthorityBoundary: It cannot select scope or version, accept risk, approve a Manifest, satisfy a privileged gate, or trigger publication.

retirement.lostBehavior: Automatic first-issue and first-pull-request welcome messages.
retirement.impact: Contributors may not receive immediate acknowledgement.
retirement.residualRisk: A delayed human response may reduce contributor clarity, but cannot be mistaken for approval.

responsibilities.description: Warn when proposed changes touch RFC-required or wire-format-sensitive paths.

historicalEvidence.observedBehavior: The retired configuration identified RFC-required and wire-format-warning path groups.
The public policy requires RFCs for protocol and wire-format changes.

responsibilities.failureImpact: A compatibility-sensitive change can bypass the public RFC and review route.

responsibilities.description: Preserve the human-operable knowledge needed when retired automation, CI, or provider state cannot be trusted as authority.

historicalEvidence.observedBehavior: The retired LLM integration and all listed features were disabled.
The contributor guide records local build, test, clippy, and formatting commands.
The public governance document identifies the BDFL decision and RFC route.

responsibilities.failureImpact: A maintainer may confuse historical automation or green CI with authority and lack a documented manual fallback.
